data_leakage_policy: Ground-truth LLR text is filtered to the target allocation, used only by local evaluation/refinement, and is never inserted into generation prompts or the final prompt.
initial_prompt:
Create low-level requirements for exactly one target subsystem.

Scope rules:
- Generate only singular, atomic low-level requirements that belong to the target subsystem.
- Do not generate requirements for any other subsystem, even if the HLR could involve them.
- Every returned allocation value must be exactly "{TARGET_ALLOCATION}".
- Return at least one low-level requirement for the target subsystem.

Return JSON only, with no Markdown or explanatory prose, using exactly this schema:
{"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"{TARGET_ALLOCATION}","requirement":"requirement text"}]}

Allocation catalogue:
- CAS = Configuration & Administration Service
- CAUI = Configuration & Administration User Interface
- CS = Collector Services
- DOG = Watchdog
- EMC = Environmental Metadata Cache
- EMS = Environmental Metadata Services
- MEUI = Manual Entry User Interface
- QEDC = Qualified Environmental Data
- QEDS = Qualified Environmental Data Services
- QChS = Quality Checking Services
- SS = Schedule Service

High-level requirement:
{HLR}

Target allocation code: {TARGET_ALLOCATION}
Target subsystem: {TARGET_SUBSYSTEM}

High-level requirement:
{HLR}
Target allocation code: {TARGET_ALLOCATION}
Target subsystem: {TARGET_SUBSYSTEM}
Prompt refinement guidance:
- Cover every distinct behavior, condition, and constraint expressed by the HLR.
- Do not add capabilities, technologies, constraints, or subsystem behavior not justified by the HLR.
- Split compound requirements into separate atomic, testable LLRs when they describe different responsibilities.
- Generate as many LLRs as necessary; do not force exactly one LLR when the HLR contains multiple responsibilities.
- Keep the prompt stable when the prediction already matches the reference well.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Schedule Service shall, upon receiving a notification that new data is available, immediately schedule a quality checking process for that data set.

The Schedule Service shall prioritize the scheduling of quality checking processes over any lower-priority tasks in its queue to ensure the process starts as soon as possible after data availability notification.
The Schedule Service shall support concurrent scheduling of quality checking processes for multiple data sets that become available at the same time.

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall store a unique identifier for each quality checking method applied during derivation of quality checking information.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Environmental Metadata Cache shall use a standard JSON format for cached metadata records to reduce integration effort for users and providers.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Qualified Environmental Data Services shall process incoming environmental data records with a median latency of less than 100 milliseconds from receipt to dissemination-ready state.

The Qualified Environmental Data Services shall support concurrent processing of at least 10 incoming data streams to parallelize dissemination preparation.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The QEDS shall receive a scheduled request containing a schedule identifier and dissemination parameters.
